skill.name: frontend-components
skill.description: Guides AI assistants when creating, modifying, documenting, and refactoring reusable frontend UI components across React, Vue, Svelte, and web component projects.
skill.summary: Build focused, reusable frontend components with clear props, encapsulation, and practical state boundaries.
skill.author: EIS-ITS

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and path traversal patterns. The weak cryptography alerts are false positives from normal frontend component wording, while the relative Markdown link on SKILL.md line 29 is a low boundary and portability concern. No evidence found of prompt injection, malicious intent, network access, secret access, executable scripts, or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Link Escapes Skill Directory
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 29 links to ../../../agent-os/standards/frontend/components.md. This is a Markdown documentation reference, not executable code, but it points outside the skill directory and may fail or access unintended repository context when packaged.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path traversal sequence is directly present in the Markdown link. Risk is low because it references a specific documentation file and there is no command execution or sensitive path.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static alerts on SKILL.md lines 3, 17, and 24 appear to match substrings in ordinary words such as component and components. No cryptographic API, hash function call, encryption logic, or password handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose about frontend components only. There is no evidence of weak algorithms such as MD5, SHA-1, DES, or RC4 being used.

content.value_statement: Frontend teams need consistent components that stay small, clear, and easy to reuse. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to design component APIs, state boundaries, and composition patterns.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation and modification of React, Vue, Svelte, and web components.
Supports defining props, interfaces, callbacks, events, and component communication.
Encourages single responsibility components with clear encapsulation.
Applies to component state, lifecycle hooks, effects, and render performance.
Helps refactor large UI files into smaller reusable building blocks.
Supports component documentation and API usage guidance.
limitations: It provides guidance only and does not include reusable component source files.
It does not replace project-specific design tokens, accessibility rules, or review standards.
It may need adaptation for frameworks or styling systems not listed in the skill.
It references an external repository standards file that may not be packaged with the skill.

use_cases.title: Create a New Component
use_cases.description: Define a focused component with props, state boundaries, and expected composition points.

use_cases.title: Refactor a Large UI File
use_cases.description: Split a complex screen into smaller components with clear ownership and minimal shared state.

use_cases.title: Document a Component API
use_cases.description: Describe component usage, required props, optional behavior, events, and supported variants.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Simple Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a reusable frontend component for this UI need. Identify its responsibility, props, state needs, and composition options before writing code.

prompt_templates.title: Review Component Boundaries
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this component for single responsibility, prop clarity, unnecessary state, and composition issues. Recommend focused changes.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor UI Into Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this large frontend file into smaller reusable components. Preserve behavior and explain the new component responsibilities.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Component Library Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a component library pattern for these related UI elements. Define shared conventions, extension points, naming, documentation, and performance considerations.

output_examples.input: Design a reusable modal component for account settings.
output_examples.output: A component plan with responsibility, required props, optional actions, controlled state guidance, accessibility notes, and composition recommendations.

output_examples.input: Review this large dashboard component for refactoring.
output: A list of smaller component candidates with clear responsibilities.
State that should remain in the parent versus state that can move down.
Risk notes for behavior preservation and render performance.

output_examples.input: Document a button component for a design system.
output_examples.output: A user-facing API summary that covers variants, sizes, disabled state, loading behavior, event handling, and common usage mistakes.

best_practices: Start by defining the component responsibility before choosing props or state.
Keep state close to the component that owns the interaction.
Document required props, optional behavior, events, and accessibility expectations.
anti_patterns: Do not create one component that owns unrelated layout, data fetching, and interaction logic.
Do not add props for every future possibility before a real use case exists.
Do not hide important state changes inside unclear callbacks or implicit side effects.

faq.question: Which frontend frameworks does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers React, Vue, Svelte, and web components as stated in the skill instructions.

faq.question: Can it generate production-ready component code?
faq.answer: It can guide code generation, but teams should still run tests, accessibility checks, and design review.

faq.question: Does it include design system assets?
faq.answer: No. It provides component design guidance, not icons, tokens, themes, or ready-made component files.

faq.question: Is it useful for refactoring existing components?
faq.answer: Yes. It explicitly supports splitting large components and improving props, state, lifecycle logic, and documentation.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No evidence found of network access in the scanned skill file.

faq.question: What should users check before publishing it?
faq.answer: Users should confirm that the linked external standards file is available or move the guidance into the skill package.
